title: How a British amateur recorded Fly Me to the Moon for Gainax
display_title: How a British amateur recorded Fly Me to the Moon for Gainax
wikitext:
[]: How a British amateur recorded Fly Me to the Moon for Gainax             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Jun 05, 2007 3:25 pm I was looking around for information on the Eva OSTs to make sure I had the artists and track names right, and I did a search on the singer of "Fly Me to the Moon," since Wikipedia only has her name. Turns out she has a myspace page, where she's posted a little background on how she was selected to sing Eva's ED: http://www.myspace.com/clairelittley

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 05, 2007 4:05 pm A Manga TV series. Huh. That's what her Japanese friend called it?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jun 05, 2007 4:08 pm Interesting, thanks for sharing. Manga TV series huh? That's a first.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 05, 2007 5:23 pm Manga TV series?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 05, 2007 5:34 pm Claire has a Myspace page? Niiiice 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Tue Jun 05, 2007 10:09 pm Nice find! Been wanting to know who she was!

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jun 06, 2007 7:14 am cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 06, 2007 1:26 pm Here's a pic of Claire from the now gone Purlace website (Abosolute Evangelion Image Gallery), the Single for Fly Me to the Moon. EDIT: Apparently, this pic is also on her Myspace. Now if only we could get more information on Arianne as well as Loren & Mash... :twisted: (I *think* I might know what Arianne's last name is, as when I listen to EYED on my comp, Realplayer automatically has her name up, though I think when I listen to Komm Susser Tod either it doesn't have her name or gives her a different last name! :shock: ) I'll do a search sometime this weekend and post my findings. EDIT #2: Apparently, her name is Arianne Schreiber, and she does stuff with Electronica, if it is THE Arianne, that is.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Jun 06, 2007 3:13 pm Claire's version is really cool! It's like a British Rei! Well...a little more soulful. Anyways, I WUV it!

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Jun 06, 2007 4:03 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Claire's version is really cool! It's like a British Rei! Well...a little more soulful. Anyways, I WUV it!

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Thu Jun 07, 2007 11:12 am Yeah! Moar info on Arianne and Loren & Mash would be cool!

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Thu Jun 07, 2007 11:14 am Opteron-O3 wrote: TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Claire's version is really cool! It's like a British Rei! Well...a little more soulful. Anyways, I WUV it!

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Thu Jun 07, 2007 12:35 pm Executor wrote: Opteron-O3 wrote: TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Claire's version is really cool! It's like a British Rei! Well...a little more soulful. Anyways, I WUV it!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 07, 2007 1:32 pm Last chance this thread, kiddies

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Thu Jun 07, 2007 5:56 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: EDIT #2: Apparently, her name is Arianne Schreiber, and she does stuff with Electronica, if it is THE Arianne, that is.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Jun 07, 2007 10:12 pm I like the Frank Sinatra version.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jun 07, 2007 10:19 pm slothen wrote: I like the Frank Sinatra version.

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Thu Jun 07, 2007 11:16 pm NAveryW wrote: slothen wrote: I like the Frank Sinatra version.
